title: Senior Product Manager, Claude
score_rationale: Strong AI/ML domain alignment — your search platform experience maps directly to building LLM-powered products. Samsung and Amazon search roles show exactly the data-driven PM profile Anthropic looks for. Minor gap: no direct safety/alignment background, but quantified ML impact covers 80% of requirements.
recommendation_reason: Top-tier AI company with direct domain match; your search + ML PM background is a strong fit for Claude product work.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years leading AI/ML-powered search and discovery products at scale (10M+ MAU, $2B+ GMV impact). Track record shipping data-driven features that measurably improve user outcomes through ML and experimentation.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
$2B+ GMV search relevance at Amazon, shipped with cross-functional ML teams
Built real-time personalization engine increasing conversion by 18%
gap_handling: Acknowledge no direct safety research background; emphasize genuine interest in responsible AI and frame LLM product work as adjacent context.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience maps directly onto Airbnb's 8+ year requirement, and her core body of work — hybrid AI search, product discovery, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) — is almost a blueprint for what this role demands. The domain (consumer discovery, AI-powered browsing, relevance models) is Riley's primary archetype, and her e-commerce growth background at Kolon adds depth in funnel conversion and lifecycle growth. The one gap is that Airbnb's role has heavier emphasis on consumer lifestyle/travel brand storytelling, lifecycle/growth marketing partnerships, and multi-vertical strategy (Homes, Experiences, Hotels) versus Riley's B2C e-commerce and electronics retail context — but these are translatable, not disqualifying.
recommendation_reason: Strong archetype match on AI-powered discovery and personalization at scale with 9 years of directly relevant experience; domain gap is translatable and the overall fit is compelling.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization systems that drive measurable growth at scale — including a hybrid AI search platform serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. Proven cross-functional leader with a track record of shipping relevance-driven discovery experiences, recommendation systems, and conversational AI features that increase engagement, conversion, and user satisfaction.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
gap_handling: The role emphasizes consumer travel lifestyle storytelling, lifecycle/growth marketing collaboration, and multi-vertical product strategy (Homes, Experiences, Hotels, Luxe). In your cover letter and interviews, bridge explicitly: frame Samsung.com's international marketplace expansion as multi-vertical discovery strategy, and highlight the CDP + CRM personalization work at Kolon as evidence of lifecycle marketing partnership. Avoid defaulting to e-commerce jargon — translate "PDP" and "add-to-cart" into guest journey and booking funnel language.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of AI/ML product experience — including a shipped conversational AI chat experience at Samsung.com, LLM/AEO pipelines, and recommendation systems — maps closely to Klaviyo's Customer Agent requirements around AI-native product development, RAG/retrieval systems, and agent configuration. The role's emphasis on B2C brand-facing AI agents and growth/activation loops aligns well with Riley's e-commerce and consumer AI background, though the B2B SaaS configuration layer (brand onboarding, persona definition, knowledge ingestion) is a new surface that Riley hasn't explicitly owned. The location is a potential friction point (Boston vs. Riley's Bellevue base), but the role may support remote depending on Klaviyo's policy.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and shipped conversational AI experience directly match the core mandate; tailoring the resume to highlight agent/LLM work and B2B onboarding instincts clears the bar for a serious application.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-native experiences — from conversational AI and hybrid search to LLM-driven pipelines — that drive measurable engagement and revenue at scale. Proven track record of building and iterating on complex AI systems with ML/data science partners, with deep instincts for making intelligent products intuitive for both brands and end consumers.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience at Samsung.com, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is B2B SaaS platform ownership — specifically brand-side agent configuration systems (persona definition, knowledge ingestion, workflow creation). Riley should address this directly in the cover letter by drawing a parallel to her AEO pipeline work (structured data ingestion, catalog metadata extraction) and the Amazon B2B listing tool (multi-modal classification, self-serve workflows for 2M+ sellers), framing them as analogous "brand configuration" and "zero-to-one tooling" experiences.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's experience is a strong domain match — AI-powered search, ranking optimization, relevance metrics, and B2B platform work all align tightly with Faire's core needs. However, the role is titled Staff PM (one level above Riley's target of Senior/PM III), requires 7+ years specifically in complex search systems at high-growth startups, and Faire's context is B2B wholesale marketplace rather than consumer e-commerce, which is a moderate domain shift. The seniority stretch and the hybrid NYC/SF in-office requirement (3 days/week) with no remote option also add friction.
recommendation_reason: Excellent domain alignment on search algorithms and ML, but Staff-level seniority is a stretch and in-office NYC/SF requirement creates location friction for a Bellevue-based candidate.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years driving AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale, including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and relevance metric design for 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to ship search algorithm improvements — precision@3 +30%, 1st-result CTR +45% — with deep experience in B2B catalog structures and e-commerce discovery at Amazon and Samsung.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, with multi-modal classification pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and 7+ years specifically in search at high-growth startups are the key gaps. In your cover letter and resume summary, explicitly frame your combined Samsung + Amazon tenure as continuous search/discovery system ownership, and emphasize the complexity and scale (10M+ MAU, 2M+ B2B sellers) rather than company size. Lean into the B2B angle from Amazon to bridge to Faire's wholesale marketplace context.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong experimentation track record, and technical depth (search ranking, LLM pipelines, AEO) align well with Anthropic's AI-forward mission and analytical requirements. However, the role is explicitly Growth-focused on a developer/API platform — a B2B/developer tooling archetype — and Riley's experience skews heavily toward consumer e-commerce search and discovery rather than developer platforms, onboarding funnels, or usage-based monetization. The 6+ years requirement is met comfortably at 9 years, and the Amazon B2B stint provides some relevant platform signal, though it was seller-tooling rather than API/developer experience.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI credentials and analytical chops clear the bar, but the developer platform / API growth archetype is a stretch from Riley's consumer e-commerce core — tailoring is essential to bridge the gap.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth across AI-powered platforms — from search ranking and conversational AI at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) to ML-driven automation tools for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon. Proven track record of owning end-to-end product funnels, defining KPIs, and running rigorous A/B experiments to accelerate acquisition, activation, and monetization at scale.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories (Amazon)

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The biggest gap is developer platform / API growth experience — Riley has never owned a developer-facing product or API console. To address this: (1) In the cover letter, explicitly connect the Amazon B2B seller tools role to developer/technical-user onboarding and self-serve workflows. (2) Reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as evidence of shipping API-adjacent, technically complex products. (3) Highlight any personal use of Claude API or LLM developer tools to demonstrate genuine platform empathy.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's experience shipping conversational AI, RAG-adjacent LLM pipelines (AEO pipeline), and AI-powered search at Samsung maps closely to this role's core ask of building intelligent agent backends — and the Amazon platform work demonstrates cross-functional, backend-service thinking. However, the role is titled "Principal PM" and explicitly requires 8+ years with a focus on backend services and internal platforms, which is a stretch above Riley's "Senior/PM III" target seniority, and the developer-tooling/CPaaS domain (Twilio Console, API infrastructure, SID error handling) is meaningfully different from e-commerce discovery. The RAG pipeline and proactive "nudge" orchestration experience would need to be carefully reframed, as Riley's AEO pipeline and conversational AI work are the closest analogs but aren't a perfect 1:1 match.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product signal and conversational AI evidence are compelling, but the Principal-level seniority and developer-platform domain are meaningful stretches that require deliberate positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to automated knowledge pipelines — that drive measurable outcomes at scale. Proven track record designing AI orchestration strategies, defining evaluation frameworks, and embedding intelligent experiences into high-traffic consumer and platform products.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The biggest gap is backend/platform PM depth and the developer-tooling domain. Address this by explicitly calling out the AEO pipeline as a "knowledge ingestion and structured data pipeline" and reframing the Samsung conversational AI work in terms of agent orchestration, context-awareness, and latency/quality tradeoffs — language that maps directly to Twilio's JD. Acknowledge in interviews that you're stepping up to Principal level and lean into the breadth of cross-functional leadership (10M+ monthly users, global expansion) as evidence of principal-caliber scope.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, personalization, conversational AI, and LLM-driven pipelines maps well onto Airbnb's AI Personalization mission — particularly the Samsung conversational AI chat experience, recommendation API, and hybrid search ranking work. However, this is a Staff-level Platform PM role (one clear step above Riley's target Senior/PM III level), and the JD specifically emphasizes full-stack platform ownership, contextual retrieval architecture (RAG), and customer support domain expertise — none of which Riley has demonstrated explicitly. The role is also deeply platform-oriented (infrastructure, signals schemas, prompt optimization), whereas Riley's strongest evidence skews toward consumer-facing discovery and e-commerce growth outcomes.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/personalization signal and remote-eligible location are compelling, but the Staff-level seniority and CS platform infrastructure focus are meaningful gaps that require deliberate resume framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building personalization, search, and conversational AI systems at consumer scale — including LLM-driven pipelines, recommendation APIs, and hybrid search ranking used by 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record owning end-to-end AI product strategy, defining relevance and engagement metrics, and partnering with ML/data science teams to ship measurable, platform-level impact across international surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, boosting AOV by 15% — demonstrating full-stack ownership from model design to consumer-facing surface.
gap_handling: The JD asks for explicit platform PM experience (owning signals schemas, retrieval architectures, RAG systems). Riley should reframe the AEO pipeline bullet and recommendation API work as "platform-layer" decisions — emphasizing the infrastructure, schema design, and extensibility aspects rather than just the consumer outcome. If Riley has any experience with RAG or vector search tooling (even evaluative), that should surface explicitly.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and solid growth/conversion experience that partially maps to this role, but the core territory — Help Center redesign, CustOps tooling, knowledge base, and support deflection — is a domain Riley has not worked in directly. The role's AI application focus is primarily self-service/support AI (chatbots, deflection), which is adjacent to Riley's conversational AI work at Samsung but not a direct match. The NYC hybrid requirement is workable from Riley's preferred locations, but the role skews more toward support-led growth than search/discovery/e-commerce, which are Riley's core strengths.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and growth credentials transfer, but no direct support/help-center domain experience creates a meaningful gap for this specialized role.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered discovery and conversational experiences at scale, including a conversational AI chat product and personalized self-service funnels serving 10M+ monthly users. Brings a growth mindset and proven track record of using AI, A/B testing, and behavioral data to reduce friction, improve engagement, and drive measurable conversion outcomes across complex, cross-functional environments.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%.
gap_handling: Riley has no direct Help Center, CustOps tooling, or support deflection experience. To bridge this gap, the cover letter and resume should explicitly reframe the Samsung conversational AI chat feature as a "user self-service and deflection" tool, and the AEO pipeline as a structured-data-driven approach to answering user queries at scale — both of which map conceptually to what Squarespace is building. Acknowledge the pivot in the cover letter and lean into the "massive platform redesign" framing, citing the Samsung.com search vision/roadmap work as a comparable large-scale discovery-led product transformation.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, AEO, conversational AI — that align meaningfully with Lyft's AI platform mandate. However, this role is specifically focused on an internal AI Platform (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, platform adoption/evangelism, SaaS vendor management) rather than consumer-facing search or e-commerce discovery, which is where Riley's evidence is concentrated. The "Staff PM" title is also a stretch above Riley's target seniority of Senior/PM III, and the role requires deep agentic AI platform experience and hands-on ML familiarity (PyTorch, multi-agent orchestration) that Riley only partially demonstrates.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product background is relevant, but internal platform/agentic AI focus and Staff-level seniority make this a stretch that requires significant resume tailoring to bridge the gap.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — from LLM-driven pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and recommendation platforms — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record leading technically complex AI products end-to-end, defining KPI frameworks, and aligning cross-functional teams and senior leadership on platform investments at scale.
top_bullets: Defined and launched AEO strategy and automated metadata pipeline (PIM → schema markup) using LLM-driven structured data extraction, boosting AI agent traffic 8x and conversion +40% in 12 weeks — directly demonstrating agentic AI product delivery.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with ML engineers to define relevance metrics, instrument experiments, and drive executive alignment for global expansion (+20% CTR).

Developed personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase signals — demonstrating ability to scope, spec, and ship ML platform capabilities consumed by downstream product surfaces.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform/infra PM experience: Lyft wants someone who has owned an *internal AI platform* — managing adoption, evangelism, build-vs-buy, and vendor contracts across teams — not just shipped AI-powered consumer features. In your resume and cover letter, reframe your AEO pipeline, recommendation API, and conversational AI work as *platform capabilities you built and enabled others to leverage*, and call out any cross-team tooling or instrumentation work explicitly. Also be prepared to speak concretely about agentic systems and multi-agent orchestration in interviews — even if it's via recent side projects or research familiarity.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and a track record of shipping AI-powered systems at scale — directly relevant to Anthropic's mission — but the Claude Code role is specifically a developer tools / dev tooling PM role that requires at least 1 year of professional engineering experience, which is not evidenced anywhere in Riley's resume. The domain is "agentic coding tools for developers," which is a significant pivot from Riley's core of e-commerce search and discovery. The AI depth is compelling, but the dev tools archetype and engineering background requirement are the two hard gaps that limit fit.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and sponsorship match, but the required engineering background and dev tools domain are real gaps that make this a stretch application.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — including conversational AI, agentic pipelines, and model-driven search — at Samsung and Amazon. Passionate about translating frontier model capabilities into intuitive developer-facing experiences that drive adoption and measurable impact at scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Address the engineering background gap head-on in the cover letter: highlight hands-on technical work (SQL, BigQuery, prompt engineering, pipeline design with engineers), mention personal use and familiarity with AI coding tools like Claude Code/Cursor/Copilot, and lean on the Purdue MSBA quantitative coursework. Consider adding a "Technical Projects" section to the resume if any coding work exists.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, search & discovery expertise, and strong track record shipping AI-powered consumer experiences at Samsung and e-commerce scale at Kolon align well with Anthropic's Consumer team domain. However, this role is explicitly seeking a "former consumer founder" with 0-to-1 product development DNA and hands-on prototyping chops (e.g., Claude Code), and Riley's background skews toward optimizing existing e-commerce funnels rather than building net-new standalone consumer products from scratch. The in-office hybrid requirement in SF or NYC is also a location friction point for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain alignment but the "former founder / 0-to-1 product" requirement and SF/NYC hybrid in-office policy are notable gaps that make this a competitive stretch.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-native consumer experiences — from hybrid search and conversational AI to personalized recommendation systems — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Passionate about identifying emerging model capabilities and translating them into intuitive, high-impact products that drive measurable engagement at scale for millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The "former founder" requirement is the hardest gap to close. Lean into any entrepreneurial or 0-to-1 moments: the AEO pipeline built from scratch in 12 weeks, the conversational AI chat launched end-to-end, and the live commerce feature at Kolon all have founder-like scope. Frame these as "0-to-1 product bets" rather than optimization work. If you have any side projects, open-source tools, or prototypes built with LLMs/Claude Code, add them — even informally — as they directly signal the hands-on builder mentality Anthropic is seeking.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and solid A/B testing and analytics experience, but this role is specifically a Growth/Monetization PM role — requiring deep ownership of pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and subscription revenue mechanics — which is not Riley's primary archetype. While Riley has touched conversion rate optimization, AOV lift, and CRM retention, there is no direct resume evidence of owning pricing strategy, subscription tiering, or upgrade funnel architecture at scale. The Anthropic role demands a majority-growth-focused PM background, which Riley's resume does not clearly reflect — most years are spent in Search, Discovery, and AI features rather than monetization infrastructure.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain fit and analytics credibility, but missing the core monetization PM experience (pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion) this role explicitly requires as its primary mandate.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through data-informed experimentation, ML-powered personalization, and conversion funnel optimization across consumer and e-commerce platforms. Proven track record of launching AI-powered product experiences at scale — including recommendation systems that lifted AOV 25%, discovery funnels that reduced bounce 50%, and onboarding flows that increased first-purchase rates 30% — with the analytical rigor and cross-functional leadership Anthropic's monetization mission demands.
top_bullets: Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility
gap_handling: Riley should directly address the monetization gap in both the resume and cover letter. Reframe Kolon growth work around revenue mechanics: highlight the onboarding/first-purchase initiative as a "free-to-engaged conversion" problem, position the CDP and CRM segmentation work as subscription lifecycle management, and quantify GMV impact more prominently. If Riley has any experience with pricing experiments, tiered access, or paywall decisions — even tangentially — those should be surfaced explicitly. A cover letter should acknowledge the growth PM framing and argue that deep AI + experimentation fluency accelerates ramp on monetization mechanics faster than a growth PM without AI product depth.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, recommendation systems, and experimentation — that map well to the AI-powered query and governance vision at Datadog. However, the role is firmly rooted in the modern data stack (BI tools, semantic layers, data warehouses, analytics surfaces like dashboards/notebooks/sheets), which is a domain Riley has not directly built in. The B2B SaaS requirement is met via Amazon, but the depth of BI/analytics domain expertise Datadog is signaling as a near-hard requirement is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and B2B credentials partially offset a real gap in BI/data warehouse domain knowledge — worth applying if tailored carefully, but expect a competitive field of data-native PMs.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, hybrid search, and structured data systems at Samsung and Amazon — with a track record of turning ambiguous AI capabilities into governed, measurable product experiences at scale. Experienced in evaluation frameworks, behavioral instrumentation, and cross-functional delivery of zero-to-one AI products serving millions of users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; designed multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines that translated seller inputs into structured catalog templates across 20+ categories

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams; leveraged behavioral insights to iterate search experiences, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: Address the BI/semantic layer gap directly in a cover letter: frame your AEO structured data pipeline and search instrumentation work as analogous to semantic layer thinking (defining canonical metadata, governing data quality, ensuring consistent downstream answers). Reference familiarity with SQL, BigQuery, and Tableau as evidence you operate in the data stack — even if you haven't built BI tooling itself. If you've used tools like dbt, Looker, or Redshift personally, mention them explicitly.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%

Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core PM skills — roadmapping, cross-functional execution, A/B testing, data-driven decision making — are strong and directly transferable. However, this role centers on enterprise B2B work management software (project/task UX, complex data models, collaborative workflows), which is a meaningful domain shift from Riley's deep AI search, e-commerce discovery, and recommendation-systems background. The "complex data models and visualizations" and "human-centric UX for enterprise" emphasis at Asana doesn't align well with Riley's primary experience signals, and the role explicitly values B2B platform or enterprise software experience — Riley's Amazon B2B stint is the closest proxy but was tool-focused and relatively brief (~10 months).
recommendation_reason: Strong PM fundamentals and AI curiosity fit Asana's signals, but the enterprise work-management and complex UX/data-model domain is a tangible stretch from Riley's e-commerce/search core.
